Petition To Compel Accounting
Off Calendar Apr-22-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for Jeanne Scholtz. James Cilley for Paul Lederer (Petitioner).

Mr. Carberry reported that he had received the paper work from his client and that he expected to do the accounting within 90 days. Mr. Cilley did not object to having the matter continued for 90 days. Mr. Carberry raised the issue regarding the two properties being held by the petitioner stating that rents were being collected and that an accounting was needed. Mr. Cilley stated that this issue was not before the Court, and that Mr. Carberry's client was not entitled to an accounting as she was not a beneficiary of the trust. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). The matter was continued to July 15, 2008 for status on filing of the accounting.
